content.problemStatement:
Given an array  A1,A2,..,ANA_1, A_2, .., A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​  of size NNN and an integer KKK.

You want to choose KKK elements from array AAA such that difference between maximum and minimum element ( from chosen elements) is minimum.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2∗1062*10^62∗106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of input contains one integer TTT.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers AiA_iAi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer —  difference between maximum and minimum element.

solution.approach:
**Sort the array**: Sorting arranges the elements in increasing order, and consecutive elements in this sorted array are more likely to have the smallest differences.
**Sliding Window of Size K**: Once the array is sorted, the problem reduces to finding the minimum difference between the first and last elements of any consecutive subsequence of length `K`.
**Iterate through the array**: For each possible starting index `i`, calculate the difference between the `i-th` element and the `(i + K - 1)-th` element (the `K-th` element in the subsequence).
**Minimize the Difference**: Track the minimum of these differences.
solution.timeComplexity: Sorting the array takes O(N log N). The sliding window loop takes O(N - K) iterations, which is essentially O(N) in the worst case.
